rationale: Appropriations for ad valorem tax relief are expressly excluded from the rate-of-growth limitation under Subsection (a-1), so increasing the homestead exemption does not implicate this provision.
quote: Appropriations from state tax revenues not dedicated by this constitution that are made for the purpose of paying for ad valorem tax relief as identified by the legislature by general law are not included as appropriations for purposes of determining whether the rate of growth of appropriations exceeds the limitation prescribed by Subsection (a) of this section.
_note: No conflict; (a-1) carves out ad valorem tax relief appropriations from the spending growth cap.
